subject: Project Management
title: Lean Healthcare
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Lean Healthcare.

hook.text: Virginia Mason Medical Center in Seattle adopted Toyota's lean system in 2001. Results were dramatic.

buildup.text: They sent doctors to Toyota factories to learn lean principles — firsthand, on the factory floor.

discovery.text: Lean reduced patient waiting times by 65% and freed up 13,000 square feet of hospital space.

twist.text: Doctors resisted being compared to factory workers. Cultural change was harder than process change.

climax.text: Virginia Mason proved that lean saves lives in hospitals, not just money in factories.

punchline.text: Toyota's factory methods now heal patients.

quiz.question: What results did Virginia Mason achieve with lean?
options: Doubled the number of hospital beds
